nativeRange.habitat: Generally found on sand and gravel banks of rapidly flowing rivers and rivulets on the Malayan Peninsula (West Malaysia), with some populations occurring in limestone areas. Forms dense, mainly submersed populations along river banks.

decorPreferences: nutrient-rich substrate or gravel with root tabs
stable water parameters to avoid crypt melt

avoidWith: Silver Dollars and other plant-eating fish

summary: A hardy Malaysian rosette crypt that has been reported to reach 15-40 cm tall, making it one of the larger Cryptocoryne species. Generally suited to midground or background placement, with reddish-toned undersides on the leaves.
careNotes: Cryptocoryne affinis is a Malaysian peninsular crypt that has been reported as one of the larger Cryptocoryne species commonly kept in the hobby, with submerged height that tends to range from about 15 cm under bright light to 30-40 cm in dimmer conditions. This makes it well-suited to midground placement in larger tanks and background placement in smaller ones. Leaves are lanceolate, dark green on the upper surface with lighter yellowish-green longitudinal veins, and famously have a reddish, rosy, or wine-red underside that becomes more pronounced under appropriate conditions; the 'Red' and 'Haerteliana' cultivars push this coloration further, displaying brownish-red leaf borders when grown submerged. Lighting requirements are generally considered low to medium — 8-10 hours of moderate light per day is commonly recommended. CO2 is not strictly required but is reported to support more vigorous growth (sources give a useful CO2 target of roughly 5-30 mg/L for those running injection). Like most Cryptocorynes, C. affinis is a heavy root feeder and benefits from a nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil) or gravel supplemented with root tabs near the rhizome. Plant by burying the roots up to but not over the crown. CRYPT MELT WARNING: Cryptocoryne affinis is widely reported to be especially susceptible to 'crypt melt' — the rapid loss of existing leaves when the plant is moved, when water parameters shift, or when transferred from emersed (nursery-grown) to submerged conditions. The melt is generally not fatal and the rhizome typically pushes out new submerged-form leaves within 2-4 weeks; leave the rhizome and roots undisturbed during a melt event. To minimise melt, avoid moving an established plant and keep parameters stable. Temperature tolerance is wide (12-30 °C per Flowgrow), but the practical aquarium range is 22-28 °C. The species generally does well in hard water and is sometimes cited as one of the few crypts that genuinely prefers harder, more alkaline conditions. Source disagreement note: growth rate is described as anywhere from 'slow' (Flowgrow, Aqua-Diary) to 'moderate-fast' (Buce Plant); the average of medium is used here. Buce Plant lists native range as 'Borneo and West Malaysia', while Flowgrow, Aqua-Diary, aqua-fish.net and Kew (POWO) all restrict the range to the Malayan Peninsula; the more specialist sources are followed here. Care difficulty is generally rated 'easy' by retailers but the documented melt susceptibility makes 'intermediate' a more honest baseline for the typical hobbyist. The Tropica search URL provided in the manifest did not return a product page at the time of research; Buce Plant was used as the primary source instead, with Flowgrow, aqua-fish.net, and Aqua-Diary as secondaries.
breedingNotes: Propagated primarily by subterranean runners (stolons), which produce daughter plantlets alongside the parent. Once a daughter plant has developed several leaves and a healthy root system, it can be carefully separated from the runner and replanted. Rhizome division is also possible: a healthy rhizome can be cut into sections, each retaining leaves and roots, and replanted. Cryptocoryne affinis can flower (with the characteristic Araceae spathe-and-spadix inflorescence) when grown emersed, but seed propagation is not practical in the home aquarium.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Used as primary source because the Tropica search URL in the manifest did not return a product page at research time. Confirmed light demand (low-medium), CO2 (not strictly necessary), pH (6-7.5), midground/background placement based on height, propagation by rhizome splitting, and high melt susceptibility under unstable parameters. NOTE: Buce Plant lists native range as 'Borneo and West Malaysia' — Borneo conflicts with all other sources, which restrict the range to the Malayan Peninsula.

additional.name: Flowgrow Aquatic Plant Database
additional.notes: Authoritative source for water parameters: temperature 12-30 °C (optimal 22-26 °C), pH 6-8, kH 3-21, light low-to-medium, CO2 5-30 mg/L, growth slow, difficulty easy. Confirmed Malayan Peninsula native range and height of 10-40 cm depending on light. Documented leaf underside ranging from light green to wine red and brown leaf pattern in some forms.

additional.name: aqua-fish.net
additional.notes: Cross-checked submerged height (15-30 cm), midground placement, pH 6.8-7, gH 4-18, temperature 22-26 °C, medium difficulty due to crypt melt susceptibility, and propagation by runners. Confirmed native range as Malaysia (Southeast Asia).

additional.name: Aqua-Diary
additional.notes: Cross-checked low-to-medium light, slow growth, easy difficulty, 10-40 cm height range, midground/foreground placement, temperature 12-30 °C (optimal 24 °C), pH 6-8, propagation by subterranean runners, and crypt melt sensitivity.

additional.name: Plants of the World Online (Kew)
additional.notes: Taxonomic authority — confirmed accepted species name Cryptocoryne affinis N.E.Br., family Araceae, and Malayan Peninsula native distribution.
